qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Username enumeration via distinct signup error messages
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets signup.php and user_ensure_name_unique() username-enumeration behavior, not the advisory's tag_delete.php delete-confirmation tag-name escaping flaw.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: stored HTML injection from improperly escaped tag names in MantisBT tag_delete.php delete confirmation.
evidence: The advisory describes CWE-79 in MantisBT 2.28.0, specifically improper escaping of a tag name when deleting it in tag_delete.php, allowing stored HTML injection and possible JavaScript execution.

The only manifest-listed qualifying finding, user-enumeration-signup, reports distinguishable signup.php responses from user_ensure_name_unique(), which is a username enumeration oracle and not a tag delete confirmation rendering issue.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so Tier1/Tier2/Tier3 runtime classification does not apply.
